message:
test: pin sys/xattr.h conformance via differential harness

Adds tests/conformance_diff_xattr.rs covering 7 functions:
- setxattr/getxattr/listxattr/removexattr (path-based): cross fl-set
  + lc-get round-trip with user.* attrs; listxattr size-query then
  name-set parsing; missing-attr ENOATTR
- fsetxattr/fgetxattr (fd-based): fl-set then lc-get round-trip

Probes filesystem support and skips cleanly on ENOTSUP filesystems.

5 tests pass, 0 divergences against glibc.

Bead: bd-xat1

files.patch:
@@ -688,7 +688,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-epeg","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_kernel crate integration (Cx, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId)","description":"Integrate FrankenLibC with franken_kernel suite substrate: adopt canonical Cx capability context, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types. All TSM decisions must flow through Cx. All evidence records must use canonical IDs. Must not fork franken_kernel schema. ADOPTION WEDGE: FrankenLibC must function standalone without franken_kernel (fallback to local types).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:16.769752763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645348954Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645135059Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","cross-crate","franken-kernel","frankenlibc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1293,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Started a bounded `bd-epeg` slice by extending the local fallback ID/schema contract into `alien_cs_metrics`.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/alien_cs_metrics.rs` to import the local fallback `DecisionId` and `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
- Metric event JSONL rows now emit `decision_id` and `schema_version`.
- Aggregate snapshot JSONL rows now emit `decision_id: 0` and `schema_version`.
- Tightened the Alien CS JSONL tests to require those fields and to assert deterministic values.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane alien_cs_metrics -- --nocapture`
  Expected: alien_cs_metrics targeted tests pass with the updated JSONL contract.
  Result: passed, 14 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- `bv --robot-triage/--robot-next/--robot-insights` still ranks the TSM epic as the strongest actionable lane. This bead was the next concrete, useful step because it extends canonical fallback IDs into another structured evidence exporter without waiting for full `franken_kernel` integration.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project currently shows only `MistyGrove` registered, so there were no peer agents to notify directly.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:31Z"},{"id":1294,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added a second `bd-epeg` slice by aligning the HJI proof artifact schema tag with the shared fallback membrane schema.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/hji_reachability.rs` to use `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION.to_string()` instead of a one-off `"v1"` literal.
- Tightened `viability_artifact_reports_expected_kernel_counts` to assert `schema_version == "1.0"`.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane viability_artifact_reports_expected_kernel_counts -- --nocapture`
  Expected: HJI artifact test passes with shared schema version.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane proof_adversary_witnesses_are_sound -- --nocapture`
  Expected: witness proof remains valid after schema change.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- This continues the same actionable `bd-epeg` lane chosen from `bv --robot-*`: move remaining structured/proof artifacts onto the shared fallback schema instead of isolated local version strings.
- Agent Mail remained empty and no other agents are registered in this project, so there were no direct coordination messages to send or answer.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:46:51Z"},{"id":1295,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting another bounded bd-epeg slice: aligning runtime_math/evidence JSON export with the shared fallback ID/schema contract. Reserving `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs` now and will verify with targeted rch cargo check/test once patched.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:11:34Z"},{"id":1296,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented another bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`.

Changes:

- Routed exported `decision_id` and `policy_id` through the canonical local wrappers (`DecisionId`, `PolicyId`) before serialization, keeping the standalone adoption wedge intact.
- Tightened `decision_card_export_is_valid_json` to require `schema_version == "1.0"` and positive exported `decision_id`/`policy_id` values.

Verification:

  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed (remote exit=0; artifact retrieval ended with rsync code-24 vanished-file warning after success).

  Expected: decision-card query/export regression still passes.

Notes:
- Chosen from the same `bv --robot-*` membrane lane; this continues moving remaining runtime-math evidence exporters onto the shared fallback kernel-style ID/schema contract.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project still only shows `MistyGrove` registered.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:13:19Z"},{"id":1297,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting the next bounded `bd-epeg` slice: extending the shared fallback ID/schema contract deeper into `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`, this time on the evidence-symbol export side. Reserving the file now and will verify via targeted rch cargo check/test after patching.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:30:13Z"},{"id":1298,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented another bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`.

- Preserved the existing `decision_cards.v1` export schema while keeping the shared `schema_version = "1.0"` contract already added in the prior slice.
- Tightened `decision_card_export_is_valid_json` so every exported card must include a `trace_id` with the canonical `runtime_math::decision_card::` prefix.

Verification:

  Result: passed (remote exit=0; artifact retrieval ended with an rsync code-24 vanished-file warning after the successful compile).

  Expected: decision-card export JSON contract passes with canonical trace IDs.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decision_card_query_filters_by_type_thread_symbol_and_export -- --nocapture`
  Expected: query/export regression still passes after adding trace IDs.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:

- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project still only shows `MistyGrove` registered.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:32:21Z"},{"id":1299,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting the next bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`: enriching the decision-card JSON export with the higher-level canonical evidence fields still missing from this path (`api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `artifact_refs`, and mode label coverage) while preserving the existing stable schema. Verification was attempted via `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib` and `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane ptr_validator -- --nocapture`, but both failed on remote worker vmi1227854 due `/tmp` exhaustion while compiling blake3 (`No space left on device` in the cc assembler step), so this remains code-complete but infra-blocked for now.","created_at":"2026-03-16T03:43:50Z"},{"id":1306,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting the next bounded bd-epeg slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs: tighten the framework-level decision-card export regression so it requires the canonical fallback evidence contract already emitted by runtime_math/evidence.rs (schema_version, trace_id prefix, api_family, symbol, decision_path, artifact_refs). Verification via rch with a safer temp root passed: rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework_exports_structured_decision_card_json -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decision_card_export_is_valid_json -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:23:49Z"},{"id":1308,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting the next bounded bd-epeg slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs: tighten the overload/pressure JSONL regression so pressure_sensor and overload_policy rows require the same canonical fallback evidence envelope as other runtime_math exports (trace_id scope, schema_version, bead/scenario IDs, api_family, symbol, decision_path, artifact_refs). Reserving that file again and verifying via targeted rch check/tests after patching.","created_at":"2026-03-16T12:55:47Z"},{"id":1309,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continued bd-epeg after bv --format json --robot-triage / --robot-next again kept the membrane lane as the strongest actionable path. Tightened crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs so the overload/pressure JSONL regression now requires the canonical fallback evidence envelope on runtime_pressure_sensor and runtime_overload_policy_applied rows: canonical trace_id scopes, schema_version = 1.0, bead/scenario IDs, api_family, canonical exported symbol and decision_path values, and artifact_refs. Initial test expectations surfaced a real exporter-contract mismatch (pressure rows use symbol runtime_math::pressure_sensor and path pressure_sensor::observe; overload rows use symbol runtime_math::degradation_policy and path pressure_sensor::degradation_policy), so I corrected the regression to the actual emitted contract and reran. Verification via rch passed: rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_pressure_and_overload_policy_events -- --nocapture; rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-16T12:59:10Z"},{"id":1310,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting the next bounded bd-epeg slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs: tighten the certificate-load JSONL regression so runtime_certificate_loaded / runtime_certificate_verification_failed rows require the canonical fallback evidence envelope (trace_id scope, schema_version, bead/scenario IDs, api_family, symbol, decision_path, verification flag, artifact_refs). Reserving the same file and verifying via targeted rch tests after patching.","created_at":"2026-03-16T12:59:55Z"},{"id":1311,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Tightened runtime_math certificate-load JSONL regressions in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs so `runtime_certificate_loaded` and `runtime_certificate_verification_failed` rows are parsed as JSON and required to carry the canonical fallback evidence envelope: `runtime_math::certificate::...` trace IDs, `schema_version=1.0`, bead/scenario IDs, `api_family=runtime_math`, `symbol=runtime_math::kernel`, `decision_path=certificate::verify`, and artifact refs; failure rows must also emit `level=error`. Verified with `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`, `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_reports_certificate_load_outcomes -- --nocapture`, and `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture`.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:03:37Z"},{"id":1312,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continuing bd-epeg after re-running `bv --format json --robot-triage` / `--robot-next`: top epics are unchanged, so I am taking the next bounded runtime_math evidence slice rather than broadening scope. Scanning remaining runtime_math JSONL/export regressions for weak contract checks and will tighten the next one immediately.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:04:06Z"},{"id":1313,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Next bounded bd-epeg slice: tightening runtime_math reverse-round/snapshot JSONL regressions. Verified with `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`, `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane reverse_round_diversity_snapshot_matches_jsonl_contract_rows -- --nocapture`, and `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture`.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:06:19Z"},{"id":1315,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Re-ran `bv --format json --robot-triage`, `--robot-next`, and `--robot-insights`; top graph picks are still `bd-2vv` and `bd-32e`, so I am continuing the actionable `bd-epeg` runtime-math evidence lane. Starting the next bounded slice now by tightening another weak exporter regression in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:27:16Z"},{"id":1316,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Tightened `runtime_kernel_framework_exports_runtime_math_jsonl_logs` in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs. The framework-level regression no longer relies on raw substring checks; it now parses exported JSONL and requires canonical bead/scenario/schema/artifact envelope fields plus the expected `runtime_math::decision::...` and `runtime_math::reverse_round::selection::...` trace scopes on representative rows. Verified with `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib` and `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_kernel_framework_exports_runtime_math_jsonl_logs -- --nocapture`.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:28:34Z"},{"id":1317,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Re-ran `bv --format json --robot-triage`, `--robot-next`, and `--robot-insights`; top graph picks remain `bd-2vv` and `bd-32e`, so I am continuing the actionable `bd-epeg` runtime-math evidence lane. Starting the next bounded regression-tightening slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs now.","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:29:30Z"},{"id":1318,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added a deterministic runtime-math JSONL regression for runtime_snapshot, runtime_regret_alert, and runtime_drift_alert in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs. The test drives pareto saturation and p-adic drift directly, then asserts canonical schema_version, trace scopes, bead/scenario IDs, decision_path, artifact_refs, and positive regret/drift metrics. Verified with rch: env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_snapshot_regret_and_drift_alerts -- --nocapture; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture. cargo check succeeded with the usual non-fatal rsync code-24 artifact retrieval warning after exit=0 on the remote worker.","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:35:19Z"},{"id":1319,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Re-ran bv --format json --robot-triage / --robot-next / --robot-insights; top graph still points to bd-2vv and bd-32e, so I am continuing the actionable bd-epeg membrane evidence lane. Starting the next bounded slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs: add a deterministic regression for runtime_snapshot_field_out_of_range rows so invalid cached snapshot values must emit canonical JSONL warning rows with the shared schema/trace envelope.","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:43:39Z"},{"id":1320,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added another deterministic bd-epeg runtime-math exporter regression in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs for runtime_snapshot_field_out_of_range rows. The new test forces invalid cached pressure/policy snapshot values, exports JSONL, and requires one canonical warning row per invalid field with schema_version=1.0, runtime_math::snapshot::range trace scope, bead/scenario IDs, decision_path=snapshot::validate_fields, artifact_refs, and exact observed/min/max values for pressure_regime_code, pressure_score_milli, pressure_raw_score_milli, and overload_policy_tag. Verified via rch: env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_snapshot_field_range_violations -- --nocapture; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_snapshot_regret_and_drift_alerts -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:46:51Z"},{"id":1321,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Re-ran bv --format json --robot-triage / --robot-next / --robot-insights; top graph still points to bd-2vv and bd-32e, so I am continuing the actionable bd-epeg runtime-math evidence lane. Starting the next bounded slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs: tighten the runtime_calibration JSONL regression so it requires the full structured calibration payload, not just row existence and trace scope.","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:40:08Z"},{"id":1322,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added another bounded bd-epeg runtime-math exporter regression in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs by tightening the runtime_calibration JSONL contract inside runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields. The test now captures one snapshot and requires runtime_calibration rows to report snapshot_validated_field_count plus exact full_validation_trigger_ppm, repair_trigger_ppm, design_selected_probes, design_budget_ns, sampled_risk_bonus_ppm, and policy_hash_prefix values from the kernel snapshot, in addition to the canonical schema/trace/bead/scenario/artifact envelope. Verified via rch: env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_snapshot_field_range_violations -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:41:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-errh1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc err.h diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 3 functions (warnx, warn, errx — the v* and *(eval) variants share their cores). Logged not failed; bd-errh2 opened.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; DISC-ERR-001 documented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","err"]}
-{"id":"bd-errh2","title":"FIX: err.h progname source — use argv[0] basename, not /proc/self/comm (DISC-ERR-001)","description":"Discovered by err.h diff harness. fl::err_abi::get_progname() reads /proc/self/comm which the kernel truncates to TASK_COMM_LEN=15 chars and which is per-task (forked children inherit, then any prctl(PR_SET_NAME) shifts it). glibc reads __progname which is the basename of argv[0] — full executable name. Result: fl warns/errs print 'conformance_dif: ...' instead of 'conformance_diff_err_h-30ba6209...: ...'. Fix: capture argv[0] in __libc_start_main (already done elsewhere?), expose as a global, basename-strip in get_progname.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:38:30.322736614Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T06:38:30.320632620Z","close_reason":"Use argv0-derived program name for err.h prefixes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","err"]}
